supply_chain.dependencies: More than 5,000 primary suppliers worldwide, no single supplier accounts for more than 5% of total purchases. Products sourced from Asia and other areas increase risk due to lead times and geopolitical risks.
supply_chain.risks: Supply chain disruptions from natural disasters, extreme weather, pandemic disease, geopolitical unrest, tariffs, trade issues, detention orders, labor problems, transportation availability, supplier consolidation, inflation, cybersecurity attacks

regulatory_risks: Advertising, marketing and internet regulations including use of cookies
Anti-bribery and corruption laws
Competition and antitrust regulations
Data protection including Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard
Data privacy including California Consumer Privacy Act, Privacy Rights Act, Japan Act on Protection of Personal Information, EU General Data Protection Regulation
Cybersecurity requirements including protection of information and incident responses
Environmental protection laws including climate-related regulations, carbon pricing mechanisms, emissions trading systems
Currency exchange controls and cash repatriation restrictions
Health and safety laws
Import and export compliance including U.S. Commerce Department Export Administration Regulations, trade sanctions by Office of Foreign Asset Control, anti-money laundering regulations
Intellectual property laws
Labor laws including federal and state wage and hour laws
Product compliance or safety laws
Supplier regulations regarding sources of supplies or products
Tax laws including U.S. taxes on international subsidiaries
Unclaimed property laws
Government contract regulations related to procurement, formation and performance
Disaster or emergency declaration pricing statutes
New or stricter environmental laws or regulations including climate change disclosure requirements from SEC and California
EU Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive
EU Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive
Climate-related policies on transportation emissions

sanction_risks: Trade sanctions promulgated by Office of Foreign Asset Control
Changes in U.S. trade policy including new or additional increases in duties or tariffs
Retaliatory actions by U.S. trade partners
Geopolitical conflicts and related international responses exacerbating inflationary pressures
Escalation of geopolitical tensions across the world and potential actions taken in response including sanctions, embargoes or other trade barriers
Geopolitical unrest affecting supply chain and sourcing
